title: IIOS Capability Wire Token
description: The wire artifact presented at consumption time: a JWS Compact Serialization (RFC 7515) string. This schema validates SURFACE SHAPE ONLY — three Base64URL segments separated by two dots. It does NOT validate that the protected header decodes to governance/schemas/capability-protected-header.schema.json, that the payload decodes to governance/schemas/capability-claims.schema.json, or that the signature is cryptographically valid. Those checks are the Verification profile in docs/23_CAPABILITY_MODEL.md, run outside JSON Schema, not implemented in this change.

format.description: Fixed to the one wire format this profile supports: JWS Compact Serialization. No JWS JSON Serialization, no bespoke format.

token.description: protected.payload.signature — exactly three Base64URL (unpadded) segments separated by '.'. protected decodes to capability-protected-header.schema.json; payload decodes to the RFC 8785 JCS serialization of capability-claims.schema.json.
token.$comment: This pattern checks segment shape only (three dot-separated Base64URL groups). It cannot and does not verify that each segment decodes to valid JSON, that the decoded header/claims satisfy their respective schemas, or that the signature is valid — that is cryptographic and semantic validation, explicitly out of scope for JSON Schema and not implemented here.

$comment: Deliberately absent by design: 'algorithm' and 'key_id' as external fields (alg/kid live only inside the signed protected header, never unprotected — see docs/ADR/ADR-0011-GOVERNANCE-MVP-OWNER-DECISIONS.md), any separate 'signature' field (it is the token string's third segment, not a sibling property), and any decoded payload duplicated alongside the token (decoding is a verification-time operation, not a stored shape).
